title: BonkDAO reports $20M theft from 'malicious governance proposal'
summary: The developers behind the memecoin project said they had informed law enforcement after the attack and were working to recover funds and identify those responsible.
body:
BonkDAO, the developers behind the popular memecoin project, reported a $20 million theft following a 'malicious governance proposal.' The attackers exploited a vulnerability in the governance system to siphon funds, prompting the team to inform law enforcement and initiate recovery efforts.

The incident highlights the risks associated with decentralized governance systems. BonkDAO's developers stated they are collaborating with security experts to identify the responsible parties and retrieve the stolen assets. The team has not yet provided specific details about the nature of the exploit or the timeline for recovery.

This theft underscores the importance of robust security measures in decentralized finance (DeFi) projects. For everyday users, it serves as a reminder to stay informed about the security practices of the platforms they use and to be cautious with governance participation until more details emerge.

Users should monitor official BonkDAO channels for updates on the investigation and any potential compensation plans. The community's response and the developers' actions in the coming days will be critical in restoring trust and security.
twitterThread: BonkDAO reports $20M theft from a 'malicious governance proposal.' Law enforcement has been notified. 🚨
The developers behind the memecoin project are working to recover funds and identify those responsible. Security experts are assisting.

Stay tuned for updates from BonkDAO on the investigation. This highlights the risks in DeFi governance. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-06-bonkdao-hit-by-20m-theft-via-malicious-governance-proposal
